description: Privacy audit: captures all outbound/inbound network connections from VS Code and Windows telemetry services, traces each endpoint to its owner (WHOIS/ASN/PTR/GeoIP), maps data categories, locates local staging files, and generates a classified risk report. Read-only — makes no network changes.

commands.summary: Step 1+2 — Capture active TCP connections, resolve PIDs to process names, match against known telemetry FQDNs. Read-only.

flags.name: --resolve
flags.description: Resolve remote IPs to hostnames via PTR lookup

flags.name: --save
flags.description: Save JSON output to this file path

commands.summary: Step 3 — Trace ownership of a remote IP: WHOIS via RDAP, ASN via BGPView, PTR reverse-DNS, GeoIP via ipinfo.io. Passive lookups only.

flags.name: --ip
flags.description: Remote IP address to look up

commands.summary: Step 5 — Enumerate local telemetry staging files, ETL logs, Watson crash queues, DiagTrack registry keys. Read-only.
Step 6 — Full audit: run scan + sources, then generate the classified risk table (Process / Remote Host / Owner / Country / Data Category / Risk Level / Status).
Print the complete copy-pasteable command reference for Steps 1–5 (PowerShell, netstat, Wireshark, ETW, WMI).
